2_Location_Based_Restriction.rule: Do NOT key the APN unless the section is clearly closed or complete

6_AKA_FKA_Priority.rule: Give priority to Normal Parcel and key only the Normal Parcel

7_Multiple_APN_Same_County.rule: If multiple APNs are present within the same county, key all APNs as addendum

1_No_Sales_Price_Available.rule: If no sales price is available in the image, do not key any code

2_Sales_Price_Present.rule: If a sales price amount is present in the image, key the amount in the Sales Price field with code 'RD'

3_Conditions_For_Keying.3a_Minimum_Amount: Sales price must be greater than 100
3_Conditions_For_Keying.3c_Decimal_Handling: Key whole dollars only — ignore digits after the decimal point (e.g., 250000.15 → key as 250000)

5_HB_Code.rule: If a sales price amount is available in the image along with phrases like 'High Bid', 'Bid', or 'Bid Amount','Highest Bid','Best Bid', key the amount with code 'HB'
5_HB_Code.priority: Overrides all standard sales price rules including RD
notes: Do not use RD if bid-related wording is present
LA counties sheriff deed usually contain bid amount
Al counties foreclosure usually contain bid amount

6_CS_Code.rule: If a sales price amount is available on a Cash Sale Deed or Cash Deed (CHDE), key the amount with code 'CS'

7_8_AV_Code.rule: If no sales price is in the body of the document but an amount is present in a Real Estate Validation Form / RTT Form / RT Form / RT-1, key that amount with code 'AV'
7_8_AV_Code.common_counties: AL Montgomery, IL counties, SC counties

9_Body_vs_Form_Priority.rule: If sales price appears in both the body of the document and a Real Estate form, prioritize the body amount and use code 'RD'

10_CF_Code.rule: If a tax amount is present, key it per the KIs — the sales price is automatically grabbed with code 'CF' in most scenarios. Check the image: if an amount is mentioned, key with code 'RD'; otherwise the system keys 'CF'

11_NY_Transfer_Tax.rule: For NY counties, the tax amount is mostly keyed in the State Transfer Tax field. If County Transfer Tax is mentioned, key accordingly

12_NY_Recording_Stamp_Priority.rule: For NY counties, prioritize the sales price amount present in the recording stamp area over the body of the document

13_UN_Code_Multiple_Amounts.rule: If multiple sales price amounts are available, do not key a sales price amount — key code 'UN'

14_Greater_Less_Than_Exclusion.rule: Do not consider an amount mentioned as 'greater than' or 'less than' as the sales price amount

15_NO_Code_Unclear_Amount.rule: If the amount is mentioned as approximate, 'about', 'around', 'less than', a range (e.g., 1000 to 2000), or is unclear/unreadable, do not key the amount — key code 'NO'

16_UN_Code_Conflicting_Amounts.rule: If both a Purchase Price and a Consideration Amount are present and create confusion, do not key the amount — key code 'UN'

17_DL_Code.rule: Key the consideration or mentioned sales price amount with code 'DL'

18_NY_Index_vs_High_Bid.rule: For NY counties, if the document contains a sales price on the index page and a high bid amount, key the index page amount with code 'RD'

rule: If both a sales price amount and gift-related wording are present, key the sales price with code 'RD' only if the amount is a valid transaction value
If amount is less than or equal to 100 and gitt are present in the document keyed saleprice as blank with  Code GT
If only gift related details are present in the document, keyed saleprice as blank and saleprice code GT
conditions: Saleprice present in the document keyed if Amount must be greater than 100
Amount must represent a real consideration (not nominal)
eg: Total purchase amount: Gift showing in image will keyed as GT code with saleprice field blank
exclusions: If Validation Form states 'Gift' → Code GT
If amount is nominal or symbolic → Code GT

applicable_document_types: Fulfillment / Satisfaction / Released / Full Performance Deed
Deed for Pursuant of Land Contract
20_FD_Code.rule: Key as FLDE work with sales price code 'FD'

21_MN_Deed_Tax.rule: For MN counties, if the deed tax is $1.65 or less, do not key the sales price — use code 'NO'

22_Consideration_Under_3000.rule: If consideration is less than $3000, do not key the sales price — use code 'NO'

Legal District/Legal Municipality The District code is the first segment of the APN. If the APN is encountered, then the first segment can be used to programmatically populate fields:  Legal District and Legal Municipality.  See "District Codes" tab for table to populate these fields.

However, if APN is incomplete or absent from document, then the city/muni/township can be identified in the legal description and should be keyed based on document presentation.  See "District Codes" tab for table to populate these fields.
Recorder's Map Reference When Recorder's Map Reference is reported as "Liber 3 of Plats, Page 40" key Recorder's Map Reference Field as "LB 3 PG 40".
Buyer Mailing Address Prioritize the Tax Mailing Address when tied to the Buyer Name over other locations which reports the buyer's mailing address.

Life Estate When a Buyer is taking title pursuant to Land Title Standard 9.3 OR, the document indicates a phrase similar to:  "…for this lifetime...an unrestricted power to convey the property during his lifetime" - this is an indicator that buyer is assuming a Life Estate and should be coded with Description Code "LT" (Life Tenant), the remaining buyers should be keyed and coded based on document presentation.
Legal Description - Handling Private Claim No. If a Private Claim No. is encountered in the legal description, key into the Legal Description field preceded by Label "Private Claim:"

APN - Handling "WOL" reference If "WOL" is encountered near an APN, that APN is the parent parcel.  If this is the only APN reported on the document, key as APN and flag "P" for APN Indicator Code.  

When a document reports both a "WOL" Parent Tax Parcel No and beneath references a 2019 Tax Parcel No, then give priority to keying only the 2019 Tax Parcel (same as "New") and ignore the "WOL" Parent Tax Parcel No .

general.Dual Recording Instructions: The state of Minnesota has two recording systems:  Abstract & Torrens, and therefore, there are two different recording stamps depending upon the system the document is recorded in.  The majority of parcels are recorded in one system only, however, there are some parcels that require recordation in both systems.  Documents recorded in both systems will report two recording stamps.   See also instructions under "Additional Information" below and next tab.
